mechanism_narrative: A lower unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor markets, which increases wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns higher. Lower UNRATE values are consistently associated with positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE level vs 20-day forward returns is not consistently negative across 2-year rolling windows, or mean return in low UNRATE periods (< 6%) is not significantly higher than in high UNRATE periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, it signals highly accommodative monetary policy, compressing discount rates and encouraging risk-taking, which leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns after trigger are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over the walk-forward period, or if the trigger fails to generate a positive mean return in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) both rise simultaneously, it indicates synchronized demand and supply growth, signaling economic expansion and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns. The interaction of rising demand and supply is more informative than each series individually.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of both series rising does not produce a positive mean 20-day return significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05), or if the interaction term in a regression is not statistically significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: In an accommodative monetary policy regime defined by the federal funds rate below 1%, a further decline in FEDFUNDS reduces borrowing costs and stimulates investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns. The relationship flips or attenuates in a tightening regime.
failure_condition: If the negative relationship between FEDFUNDS and 20-day returns is not stronger when FEDFUNDS < 1% than when FEDFUNDS >= 1%, measured by a Chow test with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns. Higher INDPRO levels are consistently associated with positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between INDPRO level and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (Spearman rho < 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly payroll employment growth exceeds 150,000 jobs (PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150k), it signals strong labor demand, rising incomes, and consumer confidence, which supports equity prices over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the mean 5-day return after trigger is not positive and statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10), or if the trigger produces negative returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal robust consumer demand and residential investment, which are positive for economic growth and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter. The joint movement is a stronger signal than either individually.
failure_condition: If the composite signal of both series increasing does not predict positive 60-day returns with a mean above zero (t-test p > 0.05), or if the interaction effect is not significant in a linear regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime characterized by unemployment above 5% and declining, combined with positive real GDP growth (GDPC1), equities benefit from the improving macro outlook. Falling UNRATE and rising GDP jointly reinforce the recovery narrative, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5% and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth positive
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (UNRATE > 5% and declining, GDP growing)
failure_condition: If the joint trigger does not produce a positive mean 60-day return significantly greater than the unconditional mean (one-sided test, p > 0.10), or if the directional concordance rate (both series moving as expected) is below 60%.
